creator: tschetter.victor@gmail.com
text:
I just got an email from postmaster saying there is an issue with this port. It also specified v2.0.2, which I’ve never mentioned or pushed. We are on v2.0 on fresh ports I see.

There are also some missing things (probably because I didn’t diff against the ports tree) but I can push them in a later commit, as they are just pkg-message stuff.

But should I be worried about the port master email? Here are the contents.

Dear port maintainer,

The portscout new distfile checker has detected that one or more of your
ports appears to be out of date. Please take the opportunity to check
each of the ports listed below, and if possible and appropriate,
submit/commit an update. If any ports have already been updated, you can
safely ignore the entry.

You will not be e-mailed again for any of the port/version combinations
below.


Port                                            | Current version | New version
------------------------------------------------+-----------------+------------
sysutils/zfs-replicate                          | 2.0             | v2.0.2
------------------------------------------------+-----------------+------------
